api/spec/packages/legacy/src/billing.purpose: TypeSpec schema-only folder defining the v1 billing API surface: billing profiles, customer overrides, and invoice lifecycle endpoints. All files compile into api/openapi.yaml via `make gen-api`; no runtime code lives here.

patterns.name: @friendlyName on every model, union, enum, and interface
patterns.description: Every public declaration carries @friendlyName to produce stable, human-readable Go type names. Missing @friendlyName causes autogenerated names that break SDK contracts on schema change.

patterns.name: @visibility lifecycle decorators on every field
patterns.description: Fields must be annotated with Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update as appropriate. Fields without @visibility default to all lifecycles and expose internal state in create/update payloads.

patterns.name: Discriminated unions with envelope:none for polymorphic types
patterns.description: All union variants use @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) so the discriminator field is inlined rather than nested in an envelope object.

patterns.name: Spread (...) for model composition instead of inheritance (extends)
patterns.description: Models reuse shared fields via spread (e.g. ...global.Resource, ...ResourceTimestamps, ...OmitProperties<T, ...>). Using extends where spread is expected changes property semantics and breaks generated SDK types.

patterns.name: Explicit @operationId on every interface operation
patterns.description: Every operation must carry @operationId to produce stable Go function names in the generated server stub (api/api.gen.go). Omitting it causes autogenerated names that may collide or change across TypeSpec compiler versions.

patterns.name: Namespace OpenMeter on all declarations
patterns.description: Every model, union, enum, and interface must be declared inside namespace OpenMeter. Missing namespace causes the symbol to land in the global scope and fail to compile as part of the package.

patterns.name: main.tsp as the sole entry point importing all sibling files
patterns.description: The package's main.tsp imports the invoices/main.tsp child and every sibling .tsp file. A new file added without an import in main.tsp compiles in isolation but is invisible to the full package output.

key_file_guides.role: Package entry point: imports all sibling and child .tsp files and declares the OpenMeter namespace usage directives.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new .tsp file without a matching import here — the file silently produces no output.

key_file_guides.role: Defines BillingProfile, BillingProfileCreate, BillingProfileReplaceUpdate, BillingWorkflow and its sub-settings (collection, invoicing, payment, tax), the BillingProfileApps / BillingProfileAppReferences union, and the CRUD interface for /api/v1/billing/profiles.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BillingProfileReplaceUpdate uses `is TypeSpec.Rest.Resource.ResourceReplaceModel<BillingProfile>` — adding a field to BillingProfile without considering its visibility on the replace model will leak read-only fields into the update payload.

key_file_guides.role: Defines customer override CRUD at /api/v1/billing/customers/{customerId} and the BillingProfileCustomerOverrideWithDetails response that merges override + base profile.
key_file_guides.watch_for: BillingProfileCustomerWorkflowOverride has app fields annotated @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) only — they must never appear in create/update inputs.

key_file_guides.role: Defines invoice listing/get/delete/update/action endpoints across three interface groups (InvoicesEndpoints, InvoiceEndpoints, CustomerInvoiceEndpoints) and request/response models for all invoice actions (void, approve, advance, retry, snapshot-quantities).
key_file_guides.watch_for: VoidInvoiceLineAction is a discriminated union with envelope:none — adding a new void action type requires both a new arm in the union AND a new model with a matching `type` literal field.

key_file_guides.role: Child package entry: imports all invoice sub-models (invoice.tsp, discounts.tsp, party.tsp, pay.tsp, tax.tsp, docref.tsp). Changes to invoice entity shape go in the child package.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not define invoice entity fields in the parent invoices.tsp — that file only defines endpoints and action input/output models.

anti_patterns: Adding runtime or business logic — this folder is schema-only; all logic belongs in openmeter/billing/
Editing generated files (api/openapi.yaml, api/api.gen.go) instead of the .tsp source
Omitting @friendlyName on new models, unions, or enums — causes unstable generated Go type names that break SDK contracts
Adding fields without @visibility decorators — fields default to all lifecycles and expose internal state on create/update
Using extends for model composition instead of spread (...) or OmitProperties — changes property inheritance semantics in generated output

decisions.decision: GOBL schema as the structural baseline for invoice models with deliberate omissions documented in comments
decisions.rationale: OpenMeter invoices are serialized to GOBL format; keeping the TypeSpec schema structurally aligned with GOBL reduces conversion code in openmeter/billing/ and makes schema evolution traceable.

decisions.decision: Visibility-controlled fields instead of separate request/response types
decisions.rationale: Using @visibility(Lifecycle.Read/Create/Update) on a single model reduces duplication and ensures the OpenAPI generator derives correct request/response schemas automatically without maintaining parallel type hierarchies.

decisions.decision: Discriminated unions with envelope:none for polymorphic workflow alignment and void-action types
decisions.rationale: envelope:none inlines the discriminator field directly in the object, matching the Go domain types in openmeter/billing/ which use a `Type` string field rather than a wrapper object.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new billing workflow action endpoint with a polymorphic input

api/spec/packages/legacy/src/entitlement.purpose: TypeSpec schema-only folder defining the v1 entitlements API surface: subjects-scoped entitlement CRUD, grant management, customer access checks, and their read/create models. All v1 symbols are deprecated in favour of v2 equivalents; new work should go in the v2 sub-folder.

patterns.name: Discriminated union with envelope:none for polymorphic entitlement types
patterns.description: The Entitlement union and EntitlementCreateInputs union use @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }). A new entitlement variant requires a new arm in both unions plus a new model.

patterns.name: OmitProperties<T, "field1" | "field2"> for model composition
patterns.description: Response models are built by spreading OmitProperties over a base create-input type and adding read-only fields, avoiding full redefinition and keeping create/read models in sync.

patterns.name: #deprecated + #suppress pair for V1 symbols kept for backward compatibility
patterns.description: Every V1 model, union, and operation that has a V2 successor must carry both `#deprecated "Use V2 instead"` and `#suppress "deprecated" "reason"`. Missing either causes compiler warnings that fail `make gen-api`.

patterns.name: Explicit @operationId on every operation
patterns.description: All operations carry @operationId to ensure stable generated Go function names. Omitting it causes name instability across TypeSpec compiler upgrades.

patterns.name: main.tsp as the sole entry point importing all sibling files
patterns.description: A new .tsp file in this folder must be imported in main.tsp or it produces no output in api/openapi.yaml.

key_file_guides.role: Package entry point: imports all sibling .tsp files including the cross-package features.tsp from productcatalog. No model or interface declarations live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting to import a new .tsp file — it compiles in isolation but is invisible to the package output.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Entitlement discriminated union, EntitlementCreateInputs union, EntitlementSharedFields, all three per-type create-input models (metered/boolean/static), and the admin list/get endpoints at /api/v1/entitlements.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new entitlement type variant requires updating BOTH the Entitlement read union AND the EntitlementCreateInputs create union, or the discriminator will be incomplete.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all V1 subject-scoped entitlement CRUD and grant endpoints at /api/v1/subjects/{subjectIdOrKey}/entitlements. All operations are deprecated and point to V2 equivalents.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add new operations here — they belong in the v2 sub-folder. Any new @route here requires `import "@typespec/http"` and `using TypeSpec.Http` since this file already has them.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Grant, GrantCreateInput, ExpirationPeriod, ExpirationDuration, and the global /api/v1/grants admin list/void endpoints.
key_file_guides.watch_for: grant.tsp already imports `@typespec/http` and uses TypeSpec.Http — adding a new HTTP decorator is safe; but missing this import in a new file referencing these models will fail compilation.

key_file_guides.role: Defines CustomerAccess and the customer-scoped access-check endpoints (getCustomerAccess, getCustomerEntitlementValue) at /api/v1/customers/{customerIdOrKey}.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CustomerAccess.entitlements is typed as Record<EntitlementValue> — the key is a feature key string and the value is the shared EntitlementValue model; do not split this into a separate typed map without updating all SDK consumers.

anti_patterns: Adding new V1 operations or models in this folder instead of the v2 sub-folder — all V1 entitlement APIs are deprecated
Adding a new entitlement type variant to only one of the two discriminated unions (Entitlement or EntitlementCreateInputs) — both must be updated atomically
Omitting #deprecated + #suppress pair on new V1 symbols — causes compiler warnings that break `make gen-api`
Defining models without @friendlyName — autogenerated names can clash with or shadow V2 names in the same namespace
Adding HTTP decorators (@route, @get, etc.) to a new .tsp file without `import "@typespec/http"` and `using TypeSpec.Http` — causes compilation failure

decisions.decision: V1 symbols are kept but fully deprecated in favour of V2 equivalents in the v2 sub-folder
decisions.rationale: Breaking API changes require a deprecation window; keeping V1 symbols with #deprecated annotations lets the compiler warn SDK consumers while the generator still emits the endpoints for backward-compatible clients.

decisions.decision: OmitProperties composition instead of standalone read-model redefinitions
decisions.rationale: Avoids maintaining two parallel type hierarchies for create vs. read; when a create-input field changes, the read model reflects it automatically unless explicitly omitted.

decisions.decision: Separate interface groups per route prefix (SubjectEntitlementsEndpoints, CustomerEndpoints, CustomerEntitlementEndpoints, EntitlementsEndpoints)
decisions.rationale: Each interface maps to a distinct URL prefix, making it easy to see the full surface of a given route group in one place and to add operations without accidentally polluting another prefix's operation set.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new entitlement type to the V1 discriminated union (while keeping V1 pattern for backward compat)
